title: cli: use StringArray over StringSlice Part 1
body:
see commits

This is only part 1 as there are much more to go but I wanted to keep the review size reasonable.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
The following cli options have been changed so that multiple values are no longer split by comma. This allows values with comma in them to be used:
podman --module
podman --hooks-dir
podman run/create --annotation
podman run/create --env-file
podman run/create --log-opt
podman run/create --chrootdirs
podman run/create --decryption-key
podman run/create --label-file
podman run/create --device
podman run/create --device-{read,write}-iops
podman run/create --device-{read,write}-bps
podman run/create --blkio-weight-device
podman exec --env-file
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago PTAL at the test changes, I mostly went with the easy option to adapt an existing test but some options had 0 tests so I had to create some new ones.
@edsantiago Thanks for the comments, should be all resolved now if I didn't mess up the interactive rebase.
LGTM but I think you need to edit push/pull.go -- or remove the decryptionkey test change for now.
I dropped the test for now, will add it back once I get to the other flags.
LGTM. Wish we had a linter for this.

> LGTM. Wish we had a linter for this.

We can write stuff if we think it is important enough, the main issue is that there are valid use cases for StringSlice so how should a linter make this choice? Or do you want the linter to warn about every use of StringSlice and require legit users to use `//nolint`?

My inclination would be to warn on every StringSlice and require a nolint, but that may be excessive. StringArray feels like is should be the default choice and StringArray should only be used with specific, good reasons.

/lgtm
I too vote for a linter and //nolint solution.
